stemHtml: When conducting a cross-country flight within a high-pressure region at a consistent altitude of 2 500 feet and following the same route, what kind of turbulence should one anticipate when flying once in the early morning and once in the afternoon?

options.a: When flying early in the morning, increased stability still keeps the turbulent ground layer below the flight altitude of 2 000 ft.
options.b: When flying early in the afternoon. increased convection processes will smooth out any turbulence in layers up to 2 000 ft.
options.c: When flying early in the morning, strong convection from night time will reduce stability and produce turbulence up to 5 000 ft.
options.d: When flying in the afternoon, increased stability will keep the turbulent ground layer below the flight altitude of 2 000 ft.
